<tbody><tr><td>

[UserLibrary](./ts-chocolate.userlibrary.userlibrary.md)


</td><td>

Aggregates user-specific data libraries.

Unlike ChocolateLibrary (shared data), UserLibrary contains only user/installation-specific data with no built-in collections.


</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

<tr><td>

[IUserLibrary](./ts-chocolate.userlibrary.iuserlibrary.md)


</td><td>

User-specific library data (journals, sessions, inventory). Separate from shared library data (ingredients, recipes, etc.).


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[IUserLibraryCreateParams](./ts-chocolate.userlibrary.iuserlibrarycreateparams.md)


</td><td>

Parameters for creating a [UserLibrary](./ts-chocolate.userlibrary.userlibrary.md)<!-- -->.

User libraries have no built-in data - all data is user-provided.

Sources are processed in order: 1. File tree sources (in array order) 2. Pre-instantiated libraries (merged in)


</td></tr>
